content.problemStatement: Write a C program to turn the string "CIVVIX" into a Palindrome. To do this, take the last character of the string and make changes to the string as needed. Finally, print the resulting palindrome.
constraints: Length of each string is at most 100 characters
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string which is ignored by the program.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the palindrome 'CIVVIC' on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case; regardless of the provided string, the program outputs the palindrome 'CIVVIC'.
Two test cases are processed. For each, the constant palindrome 'CIVVIC' is printed on its own line.
Even empty lines are accepted as dummy input; the program still prints the required palindrome three times.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
10
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
a
hidden.expectedOutput:
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC
CIVVIC

solution.approach: The key insight is that the problem asks for a fixed palindrome 'CIVVIC' regardless of the input string. The algorithm simply prints 'CIVVIC' for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no additional space is used that scales with input size.
